data.title: Apple shuffles exec ranks in effort to improve Siri: report
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) has shuffled its executive team in an effort to improve its troubled digital voice assistant Siri, Bloomberg reported.

Mike Rockwell, who had worked on the Vision Pro, will now take over the team working on Siri, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. Rockwell will report directly to software chief Craig Federighi. Rockwell's former group, the Vision Products Group, will be led by Paul Meade, who previously ran the hardware engineering team for the Vision Pro.

In conjunction, John Giannandrea, who is Apple's senior vice president of Machine Learning and AI Strategy, will no longer have Siri under his belt. Giannandrea joined Apple in 2018 from Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) to help the iPhone maker with its artificial intelligence work.

Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Giannandrea is expected to remain at the company and work on a number of other responsibilities, including research, testing and other facets of Apple's AI work, the news outlet added. There is also a team working on robotics that reports to Gainnandrea.

Apple executive Robby Walker, who led Siri up until the recent changes, called the delays "ugly" in a recent meeting with staff

The moves have not been publicly announced and employees will become aware of them this week, the news outlet added.

The change, which was made by Chief Executive Tim Cook, comes after top executives at Apple met at an offsite meeting this week to discuss a wide range of topics, including the issues surrounding Siri. 

The switch also comes after Apple told news outlets earlier this month that the more personalized version of Siri, which it showcased at last year's developers conference and highlighted in television ads, would be delayed.

"It's going to take us longer than we thought to deliver on these features and we anticipated rolling them out in the coming year," Apple said.

Apple shares slipped fractionally in afternoon trading.

data.title: UBS considers moving HQ to avert extra capital burden - report
data.description:
UBS Group (NYSE:UBS) is weighing relocating its headquarters as Switzerland pressures the country's largest bank to hold an additional $25B in capital, a demand that could push its key capital ratio to 20% from the current 14%, Bloomberg reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter and internal calculations.

The news comes amid efforts by the Swiss government and regulators to require UBS (NYSE:UBS) to deduct the full value of its foreign subsidiaries from the capital of the parent lender, the article said, a measure that aims to avoid another collapse like Credit Suisse's two years ago.

Management believes that the group's global competitiveness would suffer should it remain in Switzerland, the people told Bloomberg, adding that UBS (NYSE:UBS) could face shareholder pressure if forced to hold far more capital than peers elsewhere.

In any case, the proposal starts its journey through the Swiss parliament with a draft for lawmakers in May, though changes are unlikely before 2028. The extended discussion period offers a wide window for striking a deal. 

UBS (UBS) shares slipped 0.5% in afternoon trading in the U.S.

data.title: Adaptimmune targets $400M sarcoma franchise peak sales by 2027
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Adaptimmune Therapeutics (ADAP) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Adrian Rawcliffe highlighted the accelerated progress of the TECELRA launch, emphasizing that the company has already established 20 authorized treatment centers out of a planned network of 30. This represents two-thirds of the planned sites, ahead of schedule.
  • TECELRA recorded product revenue of $1.2 million in Q4 2024 from two invoiced patients. In Q1 2025, 10 additional patients have been apheresed, with three more scheduled by the end of March, indicating a significant ramp-up in patient onboarding.
  • Rawcliffe expressed confidence in achieving 2025 sales projections of $25 million, supported by the early commercial success of TECELRA and robust patient demand.
  • CFO Gavin Wood noted, "We have paused spending on our preclinical programs targeting PRAME and CD70, reducing forward cash flow demands by approximately $75 million to $100 million through 2028." This follows a previously announced restructuring plan aimed at reducing costs by $300 million over the same period.
  • The company is preparing for the rolling BLA submission of Lete-cel later this year, with data from the IGNYTE-ESO pivotal trial showing a 42% overall response rate and six complete responses among 64 patients.

Outlook

  • Management maintained its projection of $400 million in peak US sales for its sarcoma franchise, driven by TECELRA and Lete-cel.
  • The company expects TECELRA's sales to accelerate quarterly in 2025. Plans for Lete-cel’s BLA submission were reaffirmed, with approval anticipated by late 2026 and first sales in 2027.

Financial Results

  • TECELRA recorded $1.2 million in Q4 2024 product revenue, representing the first commercial revenues since its launch.
  • The company has reduced its cash flow needs by pausing preclinical programs and implementing a restructuring plan, targeting cash flow breakeven by 2027.

Q&A

  • Jonathan Chang, Leerink Partners, asked about the assumptions for profitability in 2027. CEO Rawcliffe emphasized the importance of TECELRA’s and Lete-cel's ramp and the cost reduction measures in achieving this goal.
  • Peter Lawson, Barclays, inquired about capacity constraints for TECELRA manufacturing. VP John Lunger assured that there are no bottlenecks, with all released products meeting specifications and timely delivery.
  • Michael Kim, Zacks Small Cap Research, asked about potential synergies between TECELRA and Lete-cel. Commercial Officer Cintia Piccina highlighted the overlap in the treatment network and streamlined processes as key advantages.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were cautiously optimistic, focusing on TECELRA's early success and the potential for Lete-cel to expand the franchise. Concerns were limited to financial sustainability and execution risk.
  • Management displayed confidence, particularly in the scalability of TECELRA and the strategic decisions to ensure cash flow breakeven by 2027. Phrases like "we feel very confident" and "we have great traction" were indicative of this confidence.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • TECELRA's launch metrics have significantly improved from Q3 2024, with a jump from nine authorized treatment centers to 20. The number of patients apheresed increased from one in Q3 to 10 in Q4.
  • The company’s restructuring plan has advanced, with additional cost-saving measures introduced in Q4 targeting long-term financial sustainability.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from TECELRA's initial launch dynamics to its scalability and the upcoming Lete-cel submission.

Risks and Concerns

  • The company highlighted the need to secure additional financing to bridge to profitability in 2027. Management has engaged TD Cowen to explore all strategic options, including partnerships and monetization opportunities.
  • Questions from analysts pointed to uncertainties around patient conversion rates and the timing of revenue ramp-up.

Final Takeaway

Adaptimmune is progressing well with the TECELRA launch, achieving key milestones ahead of schedule and positioning itself for long-term success with its sarcoma franchise. The company’s decision to pause non-core R&D programs highlights its focus on cost efficiency and profitability by 2027. The anticipated approval of Lete-cel and robust initial metrics from TECELRA underscore management's confidence in achieving $400 million in peak US sales.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Factset targets 5% organic ASV growth midpoint for FY2025 amid banking headwinds
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: FactSet Research Systems Inc. (FDS) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Interim Head of IR welcomed Kevin Toomey as the new Head of Investor Relations to strengthen the investor engagement program. CEO Philip Snow highlighted organic ASV growth of 4.1% year-over-year, with an adjusted operating margin of 37.3% and adjusted diluted EPS of $4.28. He emphasized positive sales momentum and reaffirmed the 5% midpoint organic ASV growth guidance for fiscal 2025.
  • The company reported an increase in client base to over 8,600, with ASV retention exceeding 95% and client retention at 91%. Significant growth was observed in wealth, corporates, and hedge funds.
  • CEO Snow noted strategic wins, such as UBS selecting FactSet to power advisor desktops, positioning FactSet as a primary market data partner for half of the world’s top 20 wealth management firms.
  • Chief Revenue Officer Goran Skoko discussed the launch of Pitch Creator, which has garnered strong interest from banking clients, with over 50 opportunities and two dozen trials underway.
  • CFO Helen Shan reaffirmed the fiscal 2025 guidance and detailed cost management efforts, highlighting increased technology investments, particularly in generative AI, which accounted for over 10% of revenue in the quarter.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed the fiscal 2025 midpoint guidance for organic ASV growth at 5%, with a narrowed growth range reflecting 4.4% to 5.8%. Revenue guidance was adjusted to $2.305 billion to $2.325 billion, incorporating contributions from recent acquisitions.
  • Adjusted operating margin guidance remains at 36%-37%, and adjusted diluted EPS is maintained at $16.80 to $17.40. On a GAAP basis, operating margin is expected to range between 32%-33%, with EPS guidance at $14.80 to $15.40.
  • The company anticipates higher expenses in the second half, driven by investments in generative AI and infrastructure projects to support pipeline growth.

Financial Results

  • Organic ASV growth in the Americas was 4%, supported by strength in wealth and hedge funds, offset by mixed performance in asset management and banking.
  • EMEA organic ASV grew 3%, with gains in hedge funds and PEVC firms offset by challenges in asset management and banking.
  • Revenue increased 4.5% year-over-year to $571 million, with organic revenue up 4% to $568 million. Adjusted operating expenses rose 6.3% year-over-year, driven by higher technology and professional fees.
  • Free cash flow increased 23% year-over-year to $150 million, aided by improved accounts receivable collections and decreased income tax payables.

Q&A

  • Kelsey Zhu, Autonomous: Asked about traction and pricing for generative AI products like Pitch Creator. CEO Snow highlighted the strong reception of Pitch Creator among banks, with monetization expected to accelerate in Q3 and Q4.
  • Shlomo Rosenbaum, Stifel: Inquired about better sentiment in management's tone. CEO Snow attributed this to de-risked major renewals and visibility into second-half growth drivers.
  • Faiza Alwy, Deutsche Bank: Clarified the ASV impact of the UBS Vault deal and legacy product retirements. CRO Skoko confirmed the UBS deal was booked in Q2, with implementation starting in Q3.
  • Ashish Sabadra, RBC: Asked about pricing realization and its regional impact. CFO Shan noted price realization remained consistent year-over-year, with higher adjustments in Americas rate cards.
  • Owen Lau, Oppenheimer: Queried the impact of data feed and sales growth. Management highlighted increased demand for high-quality data solutions across hedge funds and PEVC firms.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed cautious optimism, focusing on execution risks around generative AI monetization and banking-related headwinds. Questions reflected skepticism about potential contributions from AI products and the broader economic environment.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing strategic wins and long-term growth prospects. However, responses during the Q&A revealed a measured approach to addressing uncertainties in client budgets and banking sector performance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management appeared more optimistic, citing stronger visibility into second-half growth and successful client renewals.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management tone improved, reflecting confidence in second-half growth driven by a robust pipeline and strategic initiatives like UBS and generative AI product launches.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from general macroeconomic concerns to specific growth drivers in wealth and data solutions, along with questions about generative AI monetization.
  • Organic ASV growth decelerated slightly from 4.5% in Q1 to 4.1% in Q2, impacted by lower CPI-based price increases and legacy product retirements.
  • Strategic initiatives like the UBS deal and Pitch Creator launch were highlighted as key developments not present in the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted ongoing market uncertainty, particularly in banking, with cautious client spending and hiring trends.
  • Analysts raised concerns about price realization and the potential impact of lower CPI on pricing strategies.
  • The company is mitigating risks with proactive renewals, cost management, and expansion in high-growth areas like wealth and data solutions.

Final Takeaway

FactSet demonstrated resilience in a challenging market, leveraging strategic wins in wealth and banking to drive confidence in second-half growth. While macroeconomic uncertainties persist, the company is focused on executing its robust pipeline, advancing generative AI initiatives, and maintaining cost discipline to achieve its fiscal 2025 guidance targets.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Autolus plans expansion of AUCATZYL centers in 2025 to cover 90% of U.S. patients
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Autolus Therapeutics (AUTL) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Christian Itin highlighted the strong progress made in 2024, with the successful U.S. approval and launch of AUCATZYL. The product was approved slightly ahead of schedule and without a required REMS program, reflecting its favorable safety profile. The launch has already seen 33 centers authorized to deliver AUCATZYL, covering approximately 60% of the target patient population. By year-end 2025, Autolus aims to expand this network to 60 centers, expected to reach nearly all U.S. patients.
  • Management emphasized the importance of the newly operational Nucleus manufacturing facility in the UK, which supports the commercial supply of AUCATZYL with capacity to produce up to 2,000 CAR-T therapies annually. The manufacturing process is reportedly achieving consistent and reliable outputs.
  • CFO Rob Dolski reported year-end cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaling $588 million, bolstered by a $600 million collaboration with BioNTech and public financing. Loss from operations for 2024 was $241.4 million, reflecting increased R&D and SG&A costs related to the AUCATZYL launch.

Outlook

  • Management projects further advancements in the regulatory process for obe-cel, with decisions expected in the UK and EU by the second half of 2025. Meanwhile, the company plans to announce data updates for the CARLYSLE study and the pediatric ALL study in the latter half of the year.
  • On April 23, 2025, Autolus will host an R&D event to provide further details on its pipeline and opportunities for future growth, including potential expansion of obe-cel usage into additional indications.

Financial Results

  • Net loss for the full year 2024 was $220.7 million, compared to $208.4 million in 2023. R&D expenses increased to $138.4 million, driven by higher salaries and manufacturing costs for obe-cel. SG&A expenses rose to $101.1 million due to commercialization activity.
  • The company received a $30 million milestone payment from Blackstone following AUCATZYL's FDA approval. Autolus also made a regulatory milestone payment of GBP 10 million under its UCLB license agreement.
  • Management believes current financial resources are sufficient to fund the full U.S. launch of AUCATZYL and advance pipeline development.

Q&A

  • James Shin, Deutsche Bank, asked about outpatient administration potential for AUCATZYL. CEO Itin explained that low tumor burden patients show limited immunological safety events, making outpatient use a possibility as experience with the product grows.
  • Rajan Sharma, Goldman Sachs, inquired about the timeline for expanding to 60 centers by year-end 2025. CEO Itin indicated a steady ramp-up, contingent on individual center onboarding processes and legal reviews.
  • Asthika Goonewardene, Truist, queried about time from site activation to first patient treatment. CEO Itin noted variable timelines influenced by patient readiness and center-specific processes.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed interest and optimism regarding AUCATZYL's launch and early adoption, but some questions reflected cautious curiosity about logistics, such as center ramp-up and outpatient potential.
  • Management demonstrated confidence in their commercial and operational strategy, frequently referencing the product's favorable safety profile and market positioning.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment appeared slightly more focused on execution and near-term goals, with less speculative discussion about longer-term opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from preparatory discussions for AUCATZYL's launch to execution-focused updates, including early commercialization progress and site activations.
  • Analysts' emphasis moved from regulatory approval timelines to practical implementation, such as patient access and center onboarding.
  • Management tone remained confident, with a stronger focus on operational milestones and scaling the U.S. market presence.

Risks and Concerns

  • Challenges include ensuring timely onboarding and activation of additional treatment centers to meet 2025 goals.
  • Analysts raised potential logistical risks, such as variability in patient readiness and center-specific administrative delays.
  • The company acknowledged ongoing efforts to optimize manufacturing turnaround times and streamline operational processes.

Final Takeaway

Autolus delivered a robust update on the successful launch of AUCATZYL in the U.S., highlighting its broadening market reach, strong early adoption by treatment centers, and favorable feedback regarding its safety profile. The company is positioned for significant growth in 2025, supported by its operational infrastructure and strategic financial planning, while continuing to expand obe-cel's potential applications and global regulatory approvals.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Goldman Sachs sees an opportunity to ‘buy the dip’ amid heightened uncertainty
data.description:
Goldman Sachs stated on Thursday that, despite the market entering correction territory in 2025 with the S&P 500 (SP500) falling 10% from its all-time high, the investment bank remains confident in its balanced approach and intends to seize the opportunity to "buy the dip."

Regarding the recent downturn, Goldman Sachs said: “We do not expect a US recession or bear market and would use a deepening drawdown to ‘buy the dip’. However, macro uncertainty is likely to remain elevated near-term.”

While the investment bank is not in the camp of a full blown bear market recession prediction it did still lower its year-end S&P 500 target to 6,200 from 6,500 as it cited heightened market uncertainty.

On Thursday, the benchmark S&P 500 has advanced 0.4% to 5,700 and has now risen 3.7% from its recent March 13 low and now trades 7.2% lower from its all-time high of 6,147.43.

For those looking to monitor the performance of the S&P 500, there are several exchange traded funds and mutual funds available. Below are some prominent options to consider: 

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Veon outlines 2025 revenue growth of 12%-14% amid digital services expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: VEON Ltd. (VEON) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Kaan Terzioglu highlighted VEON's 8.3% revenue growth in reported US dollar terms for 2024, reflecting strong performance in local currency terms with 14.6% revenue growth. Digital services expansion contributed significantly, with direct digital revenues growing by 63% for the year.
  • The company announced progress in its asset-light strategy, which has bolstered its financial position. VEON's net debt to EBITDA ratio improved to 1.3x as of year-end 2024.
  • VEON initiated preparations for Kyivstar’s public listing on NASDAQ, aiming to enhance visibility and profile. Kyivstar's performance included a 13.7% revenue growth in local currency terms for the year.
  • CFO Burak Ozer reported a 4.9% EBITDA growth in US dollar terms for 2024, with an underlying 12% growth in local currency terms. He noted the company's cash reserves grew to $1.7 billion by year-end.

Outlook

  • Management provided 2025 guidance projecting 12%-14% revenue growth and 13%-15% EBITDA growth in local currency terms. Capital intensity is expected to range between 17%-19%.
  • CEO Terzioglu emphasized the role of digital services, expecting them to continue driving significant growth, contributing an additional percentage point to business share each quarter.

Financial Results

  • VEON reported total revenue of $4 billion for 2024, with $998 million in revenue for Q4. Digital services accounted for 12.6% of Q4 revenues, reflecting their growing importance.
  • EBITDA for Q4 reached $408 million, a 14.1% rise in local currency terms. Adjustments for identified items indicated a like-for-like EBITDA growth of 7% in US dollar terms.
  • The company's telecom segment contributed $3.6 billion to annual revenue, while direct digital revenues amounted to $460 million, reflecting a 63% annual increase.
  • Capital intensity was 20.6% for 2024, attributed to escalated investments in Ukraine, but excluding Ukraine, the intensity aligned with guidance.

Q&A

  • David Lopes, New Street Research, inquired about 2025 growth drivers, CapEx trends, and competition. CEO Terzioglu identified direct digital revenues, especially in financial and entertainment services, as significant contributors. He anticipated lower CapEx intensity post-2025 and welcomed new competitors in Ukraine and Kazakhstan.
  • Dmitry Ivanov, Jefferies, asked about the use of proceeds from the tower deal and Eurobond plans. Management stated proceeds would support deleveraging and noted no immediate plans for Eurobond issuance but highlighted a preference for local currency liabilities.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts voiced interest in digital growth strategies and capital allocation, reflecting a slightly positive tone.
  • Management maintained a confident and forward-looking tone, emphasizing resilience and strategic execution.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone reflected sustained confidence in digital expansion and financial strength.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 reflects higher revenue and EBITDA growth targets (12%-14% and 13%-15%) compared to the revised 2024 guidance of 12%-14% and 9%-11% in local currency terms.
  • Management’s tone sustained its emphasis on digital growth and operational resilience, consistent with prior quarters.
  • Analysts focused more on digital opportunities and capital structure strategies than in the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted challenges including political unrest in Bangladesh, the cyberattack in Ukraine, and restructuring costs.
  • Analysts raised concerns about geopolitical risks and macroeconomic conditions affecting key markets like Ukraine and Pakistan.
  • VEON aims to mitigate risks through regional resilience strategies and digital transformation initiatives.

Final Takeaway

VEON’s Q4 2024 earnings call highlighted strong revenue and EBITDA growth driven by digital services expansion and effective cost management. With a robust 2025 guidance, including 12%-14% revenue growth and strategic initiatives like Kyivstar's NASDAQ listing, VEON positions itself for continued momentum amid macroeconomic and geopolitical challenges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Celldex a new overweight at Morgan Stanley on urticaria candidate
data.description:
Morgan Stanley has initiated Celldex Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CLDX) at overweight saying that barzolvolimab (barzol), its asset for chronic urticaria (CU), is poised to become a major revenue generator in a decade.

The investment bank has a $46 price target (~131% upside based on March 21 close).

Analyst Judah Frommer said that barzol is an anti-KIT monoclonal antibody that helps to reduce mast cells, adding that phase 2 data in CU showed the candidate has a "potentially market-leading efficacy profile with impressive disease control observed irrespective of prior treatment."

He projects barzol revenue of $1.3B in 2035.

Celldex's only other candidate in the clinic is the bispecific antibody CDX-622 in phase 1 for inflammation and fibrosis, though Frommer sees potential here as well.

While Frommer acknowledges the stock has not fared well over the last year -- it's down ~51% over the period -- due to side effects seen with barzol  and increasingly competitive CU therapy development, those side effects are not unique to barzol. He added there is a huge unmet need in CU which "necessitates multiple lines of therapy and informs acceptable risk/benefit."

In earlier stages of testing, barzol is under investigation for eosinophilic esophagitis, prurigo nodulatis, and atopic dermatitis.
